Validating the Simulate → Fit Routine

Inject a two-screen scattered FRB, recover it with the burstfit MCMC, and check the fitter returns the scattering tail that is actually in the data — end to end.

Fitter recovers the data’s pulse-broadening e-folding to ~25% (1445 µs fit vs 1884 µs empirical)

FRBScintillator (Pradeep+2025) → sim_fit_bridge → scattering.scat_analysis.burstfit (emcee, M2) · nu0=0.8 GHz, BW=25 MHz

The routine under test

  • Simulate — coherent two-screen FRBScintillator → scattered, scintillated dynamic spectrum I(t,ν).
  • Bridge — sim_fit_bridge maps sim output (I[t,ν], s, Hz) to the fitter convention (data[ν,t], ms, GHz, MHz).
  • Fit — burstfit MCMC, model M2 (c0, t0, γ, τ1GHz), emcee ensemble sampler.
  • Diagnose — data/model/residual, posterior, chains, recovered-vs-injected τ, ensemble clean-recovery.
  • Ground truth — nominal τs from screen geometry and an empirical exp-fit of the data’s PBF tail.

Question: does the fitter recover the scattering actually present in the simulated burst?

Stage 1 — the simulated burst

Injected screen: τs(nominal, 1/e angle) = 288 µs, νs ≈ 0.6 kHz, RP = 0.023, dt = 5.1 µs. Many scintles per channel → near-smooth spectrum (mod. index ~0.05); the tail is the pulse-broadening function.

Stage 2 — fit: data | model | residual

M2 best fit. red.χ2 ≈ 301: the smooth-spectrum model captures the gross pulse but cannot follow single-realization scintillation, which is left in the residual. Expected.

Stage 2 — posterior (corner)

M2 parameters. The single scintillated burst admits several near-equivalent smooth fits → the τ posterior is broad / multimodal.

Stage 2 — MCMC chains

Walker traces + log-prob. Gelman–Rubin ≈ 3.2, acceptance ≈ 0.35: poorly mixed on one scintillated realization — the data, not the sampler, is the limit.

Stage 3 — recovered vs injected τ

Single-burst τ(ν0) posterior is bimodal and biased high vs the nominal τs = 288 µs. A single scintillated realization is not enough to pin τ.

Stage 4 — ensemble: clean recovery

Average 16 independent screen realizations → scintillation suppressed → clean PBF. Fit τ(ν0) = 1445 µs vs a direct exp-fit of the tail = 1884 µs → ratio 0.77. The fitter recovers what is in the data.

Takeaways

  • Pipeline works end-to-end — simulate → bridge → fit → diagnostics all run on real sim output.
  • Fitter validated — on the scintillation-suppressed ensemble it recovers the burst’s actual PBF e-folding to ~25% (1445 vs 1884 µs).
  • τs ≠ PBF e-folding — empirical tail is 6.5× the nominal τs, because τs is the 1/e scattering angle time and the screen field extends to ±2σ. A definition difference, not a bug.
  • Single realization is hard — smooth M2 cannot fit one scintillated burst; recovery needs ensemble averaging (or a wider model).
  • Next — fit M3 (free α); compare the fitted PBF to an analytic screen-PBF; sweep RP from resolved to unresolved.

Reproduce: python simulation/scripts/sim_fit_validation.py
